title: cyathostemma excelsum
intro: Cyathostemma excelsum, commonly known as the "Snake Palm," is a flowering plant species that belongs to the Annonaceae family. The health benefits of Cyathostemma excelsum include its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in reducing oxidative stress, alleviating pain, and preventing infections. The therapeutic actions of this plant are attributed to its ability to modulate the immune system, exhibit analgesic and anti-arthritic effects, and display anti-cancer properties. The bioactive constituents of Cyathostemma excelsum include annonaceous acetogenins and other phenolic compounds, which have been shown to exhibit potent biological activities.
side_effects: Cyathostemma excelsum can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal issues, including nausea and vomiting, due to its toxic compounds. Ingesting large quantities of the plant can also lead to respiratory problems, such as shortness of breath and bronchitis. Additionally, improper use of cyathostemma excelsum may cause allergic reactions, including skin irritation and inflammation. Furthermore, long-term exposure to its toxic compounds can lead to liver and kidney damage.
